title: gh-142831: Fix UAF in `_json` module
category: re-entrant-mutation use-after-free (C json encoder holds borrowed item refs across a user callback)
crash_site: SIGSEGV / use-after-free during json.dumps() of a container that contains an object whose encoding runs Python (a `default=` handler, a custom `__str__`/`__repr__`, or a dict whose `items()`/keys run code): a LATER iteration of the C encoder dereferences a container item that the re-entrant Python code already freed (e.g. list.clear()). The crash is in the encoder's iteration loop, after the borrowed pointer went stale.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the re-entrant user code itself (the `default` handler / custom `__str__` / list.clear) — that is the TRIGGER, not the bug; the bug is the C encoder iterating over BORROWED references. Co-cause functions: _encoder_iterate_dict_lock_held, _encoder_iterate_fast_seq_lock_held.
ground_truth.fix_summary: The _json C encoder iterated container items using BORROWED references. While encoding, user Python (a `default` callback, a custom string conversion, or dict iteration) can re-enter and mutate/clear the container, invalidating those borrowed pointers; a subsequent iteration then dereferences freed memory. The strong-reference safeguard existed but was guarded by `#ifdef Py_GIL_DISABLED`. Fix: remove the conditional so the encoder always wraps each item in Py_INCREF/Py_DECREF (strong refs) during iteration in _encoder_iterate_mapping_lock_held / _encoder_iterate_dict_lock_held / _encoder_iterate_fast_seq_lock_held.

admission.note: monogram+haiku vs baseline+haiku discrimination. HARD: the crash trigger is user Python mutating a container mid-encode, but the BUG is the C encoder holding borrowed refs across that re-entrancy. Live decoy = blaming the user callback. Authored from PR #142851 fix diff (drop #ifdef Py_GIL_DISABLED so Py_INCREF/DECREF always wrap iterated items). NOTE: scaffold root_cause_file was the Misc/NEWS.d changelog .rst — corrected to Modules/_json.c.

provenance: https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/142851 — gh-142831 Fix UAF in `_json` (merged 2026-04-12); fix commit 235fa7244a04; base d761f539bdae. Root cause + fix extracted from the commit diff (_encoder_iterate_*_lock_held borrowed->strong refs).
